lede: A look at August 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kimi Antonelli and Rachel Bilson, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: NASA
events.desc: NASA successfully launches the Spitzer Space Telescope into space.

events.title: Aaliyah
events.desc: American singer Aaliyah and several members of her entourage are killed as their overloaded aircraft crashes shortly after takeoff from Marsh Harbour Airport, Bahamas.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The first Bombing of Berlin by the British Royal Air Force.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Japan declares war on Austria-Hungary.

events.title: Matthew Webb
events.desc: Captain Matthew Webb becomes the first person to swim across the English Channel, traveling from Dover, England, to Calais, France, in 21 hours and 45 minutes.

events.title: Seven Years' War
events.desc: Seven Years' War: Frederick II of Prussia defeats the Russian army at the Battle of Zorndorf.

events.title: Galileo Galilei
events.desc: Galileo Galilei demonstrates his first telescope to Venetian lawmakers.

events.title: Honourable Artillery Company
events.desc: The Honourable Artillery Company, the oldest surviving regiment in the British Army, and the second most senior, is formed.

events.title: Philip III of France
events.desc: Philip III, although suffering from dysentery, becomes King of France following the death of his father Louis IX, during the Eighth Crusade. His uncle, Charles I of Naples, is forced to begin peace negotiations with Muhammad I al-Mustansir, Hafsid Sultan of Tunis.

events.title: George Mouzalon
events.desc: Regent George Mouzalon and his brothers are killed during a coup headed by the aristocratic faction under Michael VIII Palaiologos, paving the way for its leader to ultimately usurp the throne of the Empire of Nicaea.

births.name: Lise Bacon
births.line: Canadian judge and politician, Deputy Premier of Quebec (died 2025)

births.name: James W. Gerard
births.line: American lawyer and diplomat, United States Ambassador to Germany (died 1951)

deaths.text: Salim Al-Huss, Lebanese statesman, 34th Prime Minister of Lebanon
George Mouzalon, regent of the Empire of Nicaea

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ginés de la Jara (or Genesius of Cartagena)
Christian feast day: Louis IX of France
